# Skill 03: Dashboard And Presentation

Use this skill when building the user-facing dashboard and executive presentation assets.

## Dashboard Objective

Make the intelligence visible. A viewer should understand within seconds that the system analyzed work orders, found repeat failures, predicted the next failures, and generated planned maintenance schedules.

## Dashboard Requirements

Create a premium single-page dashboard with:

- KPI cards with animated counters.
- Asset by problem-code heatmap.
- Timeline chart of recurring work orders.
- Prediction leaderboard with probability bars.
- PM schedule preview grid.
- Live feed or simulated ingestion activity.

Required KPI examples:

- Total Reactive WOs Analyzed
- Recurring Patterns Detected
- PM Schedules Auto-Generated
- Estimated Cost Savings
- Recurrence Predictions In Next 30 Days

## Design Direction

- Dark theme is preferred.
- Use bold, high-contrast styling.
- Use neon mint accents aligned to the CBRE palette.
- Avoid generic boilerplate layout.
- Make the dashboard feel premium and boardroom-demo ready.

Suggested libraries:

- Tailwind CSS via CDN
- Chart.js
- Font Awesome

## Signature Moments To Include

Emphasize the following interactions or visuals:

- animated pattern discovery
- regularity score callouts
- savings counter or savings clock
- one-click PM generation state change
- SHAP or feature-impact explanation area
- confidence rings or pulse effects for high-confidence patterns
- countdown to next likely failure

## Executive Presentation Objective

Create a self-contained HTML deck that can be shown directly to senior leadership.

## Presentation Technical Requirements

- standalone HTML
- smooth slide transitions
- previous and next controls
- keyboard navigation
- slide dots
- progress bar
- responsive layout
- print-friendly CSS
- animated entrances

Use the CBRE brand palette:

- `#003F2D`
- `#17E88F`
- `#FFFFFF`
- `#F5F5F5`

Use strong typography and visual hierarchy.

## Slide Storyline

The deck should cover:

1. title and positioning
2. brutal reactive reality
3. pain points
4. hidden gold in historical work orders
5. introduction to ARIA
6. intelligence pipeline
7. pattern detection in action
8. prediction engine and explainability
9. auto-generated PM schedules
10. live demo proof points
11. business value and ROI
12. beneficiary personas
13. technical architecture
14. pilot-to-production roadmap
15. competitive differentiation
16. security and compliance
17. integration ecosystem
18. team
19. call to action
20. closing slide

## Storytelling Rules

- Make the business case obvious.
- Show proof, not just claims.
- Translate technical sophistication into operational and financial impact.
- Keep the tone confident and executive-ready.
